name: Afghania

notes: Every frozen row was reconciled against Afghania's current restaurant-issued dinner and raw-marinated-meat menus for the 2811 M Street Northwest location.
The current official site publishes 103 unique section-level presentations across 12 real categories: 94 dinner presentations and nine raw marinated meats.

Only 73 frozen names remain current. Seventy-three frozen rows belong to sister restaurant Afghan Bistro, six older Afghania rows are no longer current, and all nine current raw-marinated-meat products were omitted from the frozen catalog.
Repeated names remain separate when they appear in regular, vegetarian, and vegan sections because the published formulations and fixed allergen signals differ.

Afghania publishes current item names, descriptions, modifier choices, vegan section context, and item-specific gluten-free badges, but no complete allergen matrix, complete recipes, or kitchen cross-contact policy.

Positive fields are limited to fixed published ingredient terms and mandatory named formats. Non-dairy yogurt does not produce milk, mustard greens do not produce mustard, eggplant does not produce egg, and positive wheat/gluten signals cannot contradict an item-specific gluten-free badge.

Twenty-four frozen allergen results mismatch the current source-bounded result: nine collapsed names represent current presentations with different formulations, 11 underreport current fixed signals, three overreport them, and one has both omitted and invented signals.
Fifty-nine current presentations remain unavailable where the restaurant does not publish enough positive item-level evidence; unavailable is not interpreted as allergen-free.

The frozen API merge combined Afghania with Afghan Bistro, flattened real menu sections into synthetic buckets, collapsed same-name section presentations, and omitted the entire current raw-meat menu.
The correction reads explicit official section and item hooks from both current menu surfaces, asserts 94 dinner and nine raw presentations, and preserves category plus name as the product identity.

The review distinguishes fixed ingredients from incidental language and section context, including regular yogurt versus non-dairy yogurt and named wheat formats such as bread, brioche, croutons, noodles, dumplings, turnovers, sambosa, quroti, and baklava.

The final repair is guarded by item, category, unique-identity, positive-signal, sister-row exclusion, raw-meat restoration, regular/vegan distinction, family-special signal, false-positive, and final category-order checks.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Confirmed the Georgetown Afghania identity, address, current menu navigation, and absence of a complete public allergen matrix on the home surface.
Captured and reviewed 94 current restaurant-issued dinner presentations across 11 sections with names, descriptions, modifiers, labels, and section context.
Captured and reviewed all nine current raw-marinated-meat presentations that were absent from the frozen catalog.

Full-text review of the current official home, dinner, raw-meat, and online-order surfaces found item descriptions, vegan context, and gluten-free labels but no complete allergen matrix, recipes, or cross-contact policy.

findings.summary: The frozen 152-row menu included 73 products belonging to sister restaurant Afghan Bistro, retained six stale Afghania products, and omitted all nine current raw-marinated-meat products.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt the catalog from Afghania's current location-specific official surfaces, removed all sister-location and stale rows, and restored the omitted raw-meat section.

findings.summary: The frozen merge collapsed same-name regular, vegetarian, and vegan products even when their published formulations and allergen signals differ.
findings.resolution: Preserved category plus name as identity for all 103 current presentations, including separate regular and vegan versions with source-bounded allergen fields.

findings.summary: Twenty-four applicable frozen rows disagree with current published ingredient or mandatory-format evidence, including nine names that cannot represent multiple current formulations with one result.
findings.resolution: Recomputed source-bounded signals per presentation, restored omitted wheat/gluten, milk, tree-nut, and fish evidence, removed unsupported fields, and left 59 rows unavailable where positive evidence is insufficient.

repairs.summary: Prepared and focused-tested a 103-presentation current Afghania catalog across 12 official categories with 44 positive published-signal rows, 59 unavailable rows, no sister-location contamination, nine restored raw meats, and formulation-specific regular/vegan identities.
verificationCommands: RESTAURANT_REPOSITORY_PATH=/tmp/afghania-repository.json node scripts/repair-final-generated-regressions.mjs (isolated copy; verified 103 presentations, 12 categories, 44 positive rows, 59 unavailable rows, 9 raw meats last, unique presentation ids, sister-row exclusion, and allergen sentinels)

notes: Current corrected catalog counts: 103 section-level presentations, 12 categories, 44 positive published-signal rows, and 59 accurately unavailable rows.

Focused source-count, section identity, unique-id, sister-contamination exclusion, stale-row, current-raw-meat, non-dairy-yogurt, vegan-context, gluten-free-label conflict, named-format, false-positive, all 152 frozen-row reconciliation, and isolated final repair checks passed.
